item.title: Exclusive: Swiss bank Julius Baer initiates search to replace its CFO, sources say
item.description: Julius Baer is considering replacing its ​chief financial officer, two people ‌familiar with discussions said, a step that would round ​off a shake-up in ​the private bank's top ⁠management since it racked ​up heavy losses linked to ​risky lending.

item.title: Lockheed Martin secures $4.7 billion Patriot missile interceptor production contract
item.description: Lockheed Martin said on ​Friday the U.S. government ‌awarded the defense giant a $4.7 billion preliminary contract to ​continue critical accelerated production ​of PAC-3 Missile Segment Enhancement.

item.title: VIEW March inflation report, first to show impact of war, is in line with expectations
item.description: U.S. consumer prices rose 3.3% from a year ago in March, data showed, giving investors a first glimpse of the impact on prices of the U.S.-Israeli war against Iran.

item.title: CoreWeave strikes AI cloud deal with Anthropic, shares rise
item.description: Cloud infrastructure firm CoreWeave said on Friday it has struck a deal with Anthropic to supply the AI startup ​with cloud computing capacity, sending its shares up ‌more than 5% in premarket trading.

item.title: China warns online travel platforms against automated train ticket programmes
item.description: Chinese authorities summoned seven online platforms selling train ​tickets including Trip.com , Meituan , Tongcheng ‌and Alibaba's Fliggy, warning the firms against disruptive, automated ticketing programmes.

item.title: Geospatial analytics firm HawkEye 360 files for US IPO
item.description: Geospatial analytics company HawkEye 360 ​on Friday filed for ‌an initial public offering in the United States.

item.title: Traders keep bets the fed will stay on hold all 2026
item.description: Traders on Friday kept ​bets the Federal ‌Reserve will leave interest rates ​on hold ​through the end of ⁠2026 after ​the Labor Department ​reported consumer prices surged in March, ​as expected.

item.title: US consumer prices surged in March
item.description: Consumer prices increased by the most in nearly four years as the war with Iran boosted oil prices and the pass-through from tariffs persisted, further diminishing chances for an ‌interest rate cut this year.

item.title: Glencore buys 45% stake in South Carolina aluminium recycling plant
item.description: Glencore on Friday said ​it acquired ‌a 45% stake in an ​aluminium recycling ​and remelting plant ⁠near Charleston, ​South Carolina, strengthening ​the miner's position in the U.S. ​aluminium ​supply chain.

item.title: Lithium bulls set to gatecrash Chile's copper party
item.description: Chile is set to break with precedent and open its annual global copper gathering next ​week with a day of panels devoted to lithium, as it seeks to decisively diversify beyond the red metal that has powered its economy ‌for decades.

item.title: Exclusive: Fed's Daly says oil shock means getting inflation down takes longer
item.description: San Francisco Federal Reserve President Mary Daly said the U.S. economy is fundamentally solid, the ​labor market has steadied, and monetary policy is in a "good place" -- restrictive enough to put downward pressure on inflation without undercutting the labor market.

item.title: Snap unit to use Qualcomm chips for upcoming AI smart glasses
item.description: Specs, the Snap smart glasses unit formed early this year, will use Qualcomm ​chips for its upcoming devices under a ‌multi-year deal announced on Friday, marking its first major move.

item.title: UK threatens tech bosses with jail if they fail to remove non-consensual intimate images
item.description: Britain on Friday warned tech bosses they could be held personally liable and face imprisonment if their platforms fail ​to remove intimate images shared without consent when required ‌to do so.

item.title: U.S. equity fund inflows surge on optimism over Mideast ceasefire
item.description: U.S. investors ramped up purchases of equity funds in the week to April 8, as ​hopes for a two-week Middle East ceasefire boosted ‌expectations for a reopening of the Strait of Hormuz to oil and gas flows.

item.title: Global equity fund inflows surge on Middle East ceasefire boost
item.description: Global equity fund inflows nearly doubled in the week through April 8 as a two-week Middle East ceasefire raised hopes ​of a resumption of shipments through the Strait of ‌Hormuz.

item.title: The unconventional logic behind SpaceX's $1.75 trillion price tag
item.description: Wall Street is reaching for some unusual yardsticks to price Elon Musk's SpaceX.

item.title: Morning Bid: Back from the brink?
item.description: Everything Mike Dolan and the ROI team are excited to read, watch and listen to over the weekend.

item.title: Big Tech puts financial heft behind next-gen nuclear power as AI demand surges
item.description: Big Tech is reshaping the funding landscape for new nuclear technologies as it seeks to bolster electricity supply for ​power-hungry AI data centers, inking deals that offer nuclear companies both funding and a clearer path to making money.

item.title: Warburg Pincus, Munich Re arm team up to invest in Europe's defence sector
item.description: U.S. private equity firm Warburg Pincus has launched an investment platform focused ​on Europe's defence and security ‌sector, with MEAG, the asset manager of reinsurer Munich Re , joining as an early investor.

item.title: US trade court weighs legality of Trump 10% global tariff
item.description: A U.S. trade court on Friday will consider the legality of a 10% global import tax imposed by the Trump administration, which ​several states and small businesses say sidesteps a U.S. Supreme Court ruling ‌that invalidated most of Trump's previous tariffs.

item.title: US utility stocks are off to their best start since 2019. Can they keep it up?
item.description: U.S. utility stocks marked their ​strongest start to the year since 2019, benefiting from an investor retreat from riskier assets during the Iran ‌war and strong electricity demand from firms building out artificial intelligence infrastructure.

item.title: Lufthansa strike disrupts tens of thousands of passengers as City Airlines secures pay deal
item.description: Tens ​of thousands of passengers faced travel disruption across Germany on Friday as ‌cabin crew at Lufthansa and its regional unit Lufthansa CityLine staged a day-long strike, while the group's newest subsidiary, Lufthansa City Airlines, signed its first labour agreement.

item.title: Shippers weigh unusual routes as high air cargo rates, ocean gridlock persist
item.description: Some shippers caught between high jet fuel prices and congestion in vital Middle East waterways are now looking for transport routes ‌in unexpected places, with a recent ceasefire between Iran and the United States unlikely to offer quick relief.

item.title: Crypto giant Kraken's Fed payment account sparks concerns about risks
item.description: Crypto giant Kraken's landmark Federal Reserve master account comes with restrictions aimed at mitigating risks, but it - and others likely to follow in its wake - ​could still create vulnerabilities for the U.S. financial system.

item.title: Insight: How the AI boom derailed clean‑air efforts in one of America's most polluted cities
item.description: Barbara Johnson has been fighting coal pollution for decades in her mostly Black neighborhood of North St. Louis as an organizer with Metropolitan Congregations United – one of many activist groups campaigning for cleaner air in a city that has some of the country’s dirtiest.

item.title: US stock futures subdued ahead of inflation data, Mideast in focus
item.description: U.S. stock index futures were ​largely muted on Friday as investors stuck to the sidelines ahead of a crucial ‌inflation print, while assessing the fragility of the Middle East truce.

item.title: EU and US near critical minerals deal to combat Chinese control, Bloomberg News reports
item.description: The European Union and Washington are ​closing in on an agreement to coordinate ‌on producing and securing critical minerals, Bloomberg News reported on Friday.

item.title: Apple leads global smartphone shipments in first quarter, Counterpoint says
item.description: iPhone-maker Apple ​led smartphone ‌shipments in the ​first quarter, ​growing 5% year-on-year, ⁠even ​as overall ​global shipments remained under ​pressure ​due to a ‌shortage ⁠of memory components and weak ​consumer ​sentiment, ⁠Counterpoint Research ​said ​on ⁠Friday.

item.title: Polls in Hungary and Peru next in key election year for world markets
item.description: Voters in Hungary could deliver a boon to bond investors, while Peru's weekend polls will help determine the next step for its economy, the latest in a string of key elections worldwide this year.

item.title: Daimler Truck Q1 unit sales decline on weak North America demand
item.description: Daimler ‌Truck , one of the world's largest truckmakers, on Friday ​reported a 9% ​decline in first-quarter group unit sales, weighed ⁠down by a ​sharp slowdown in North America.
